title: Mosunetuzumab Plus Polatuzumab Vedotin in Transplant-Ineligible Refractory/Relapsed Large B-Cell Lymphoma: Primary Results of the Phase III SUNMO Trial
design: Global, randomised, phase 3 trial; 50 sites in 13 countries; NCT05171647. Randomised 2:1 to Mosun-Pola (n=138) vs R-GemOx (n=70). Dual primary endpoints: centrally assessed ORR and PFS; OS key secondary. Median follow-up 23.2 months.
population: Adults with R/R LBCL (DLBCL NOS, HGBCL including double/triple-hit, FL grade 3b, transformed FL) refractory/relapsed after >=1 prior therapy, deemed ASCT-INELIGIBLE by treating physician. Median age 62; 41% >=65; median 2 prior lines (44% had only 1); 74% primary refractory or relapse within 12 months of first-line. Prior polatuzumab permitted if no PD within 12 months of last dose.
line: Second-line or later in transplant-ineligible patients (>=1 prior therapy).
key_eligibility: Previous polatuzumab vedotin permitted only if no disease progression within 12 months of last dose
HGBCL including double-/triple-hit and transformed FL included (broader histology than STARGLO)
Fully outpatient, chemotherapy-free bispecific + ADC regimen; no hospitalization required for mosunetuzumab
outcomes: Median PFS 11.5 months (Mosun-Pola) vs 3.8 months (R-GemOx); HR 0.41 (95% CI 0.3-0.6); P<0.0001. ORR 70% vs 40% (P<0.0001); CR 51% vs 24%. Interim OS 18.7 vs 13.6 months (HR 0.80; P=0.28, not yet significant). CRS in 26% of Mosun-Pola patients; grade >=2 CRS and tocilizumab use each <5%; only one grade 3 CRS, no grade 4/5, no ICANS. Lowest reported CRS among T-cell engagers. Improved patient-reported outcomes and fewer cytopenias/neuropathy vs R-GemOx.
grounds_rubric_claims: Mosun-Pola is a gentler, outpatient, chemo-free option for transplant-ineligible R/R LBCL, superior PFS/ORR vs R-GemOx
Lowest CRS of T-cell engagers (grade >=2 <5%, no ICANS) - preferred where CRS/neurotoxicity risk or CAR-T ineligibility is a concern
Prior polatuzumab within 12 months with PD is an exclusion/sequencing consideration (pola re-exposure limited)
Appropriate for CAR-T-ineligible R/R DLBCL and proposed to replace GemOx as control arm

verbatim_quotes.quote: the median PFS was significantly longer with Mosun-Pola than with R-GemOx (11.5 months [95% CI, 5.6 to 18] v 3.8 months [95% CI, 2.9 to 4.1]; hazard ratio for progression or death, 0.41 [95% CI, 0.3 to 0.6]; P < .0001).
verbatim_quotes.supports: Primary PFS benefit of Mosun-Pola over R-GemOx in transplant-ineligible R/R LBCL

verbatim_quotes.quote: the rate of grade >=2 cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and usage of tocilizumab occurred in <5% of patients
verbatim_quotes.supports: Very low CRS - grounds gentler-option rubric claim for transplant-ineligible patients

verbatim_quotes.quote: Previous treatment with polatuzumab vedotin was permitted if no disease progression (PD) occurred within 12 months of the last dose.
verbatim_quotes.supports: Sequencing constraint on prior polatuzumab exposure (eligibility rule)
